segments.text: "Joab, son of Zeruiah, knew that the king kept on thinking about Absalom. [^36]
"So Joab sent a messenger to Tekoa to bring back a wise woman who lived there. He told her,
Pretend to be a mourner. Put on clothes for mourning, and don't use any scented oils. Be like a woman who has been in mourning for the dead a long time.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Then go to the king and tell him this.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Joab told her what to say.
"When the woman from Tekoa went to see the king, she bowed facedown to the ground in respect, and said,
Please help me, Your Majesty!
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: What's the matter?
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king asked her.
Sadly I'm a widow. My husband is dead,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: she replied.
Your Majesty, I had two sons. They had a fight outside, and there was nobody there to stop them. One of them hit the other, and killed him.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Now the whole family is against me. They're saying, ‘Hand over your son who killed his brother so we can put him to death for murdering his brother. That way he won't inherit anything either!’ By doing this they would snuff out the last ember of hope I have to carry on my husband's name and family in the world.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Go on home,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king told the woman,
and I myself will make sure your case is dealt with for you.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: Thank you, Your Majesty,
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: said the woman.
I and my family will take the blame, [^37] and may Your Majesty and your family be held to be innocent.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: If anyone complains to you about it, bring him here to me, and he won't bother you again!
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king told her.
Please, Your Majesty,
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: the woman continued,
swear by the Lord your God that you will stop the person wanting to avenge the murder from making it worse by killing my son!
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: As the Lord lives,
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: he promised,
not a single hair from your son's head will fall to the ground.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Could I please ask for one other thing, Your Majesty?
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: the woman asked.
Go ahead,
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: he replied.
So why have you schemed in a similar way against the people of God?
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: the woman asked.
Since Your Majesty just decided my case by what you said, haven't you convicted yourself because you refuse to bring back the son you banished?
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Yes, we all have to die. We're like water spilled on the ground that can't be collected again. But that's not what God does. Instead he works out ways for anyone who is banished to come back home to him.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: That's why I've come to explain this to Your Majesty, because someone has frightened me. So I thought to myself, I will go and speak to the king. Perhaps he will grant my request.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Perhaps the king will listen and save me from the man who would cut off both me and my son from God's chosen people.
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: I thought: May what Your Majesty says bring me peace, for Your Majesty is able to tell the difference between good and evil, just like an angel of God. May the Lord your God be with you!
segments.speaker: The woman

segments.text: Please don't refuse to answer the question I'm about to ask,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king said to the woman.
Please ask your question, Your Majesty,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: she replied.
Is all this Joab's doing?
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king asked. The woman replied,
As you live, Your Majesty, no one can hide anything from you. Yes, it was Joab, your officer, who ordered me to do this—he told me exactly what to say.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: He did so to show the other side of the situation, but Your Majesty is as wise as an angel of God, and you know everything that happens in this country.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: "The king said to Joab,
Fine, I'll do it. Go and bring young Absalom back.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: "Joab bowed down with his face to the ground in respect, and blessed the king.
Today,
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: said Joab,
I, your servant, know that you approve of me, Your Majesty, because you have granted my request.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: "Joab went to Geshur, and brought Absalom back to Jerusalem.
"But the king gave this order,
He may return to his home, but he's not to come and see me.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: So Absalom returned to his own home, but he didn't go and see the king.
"Absalom was admired as the most handsome man in the whole of Israel. He didn't have a single blemish from head to toe.
"He cut his hair every year because it got so heavy—it weighed two hundred royal shekels.
"He had three sons, and a daughter named Tamar—a very beautiful woman.
"Absalom lived in Jerusalem for two years but was not permitted to see the king.
"Absalom called Joab to arrange for him to see the king, but Joab refused to come. Absalom called Joab again, but Joab still wouldn't come.
"So Absalom told his servants,
Look, Joab's field is next to mine, and he has barley growing there. Go and set it on fire!
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: Absalom's servants went and set the field on fire.
"Joab went to Absalom's house and asked
Why did your servants set my field on fire?
segments.speaker: Joab went to Absalom's house and

segments.text: Look here,
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: said Absalom,

I sent for you, saying, ‘Come here. I want you to go to the king and ask: Why did I bother coming back from Geshur? It would have been better for me to stay there.’ So go and arrange for me to see the king, and if I'm guilty of anything, he can kill me.
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: "So Joab went and told the king what Absalom had said. Then David summoned Absalom, who came and bowed down with his face to the ground before him in respect. Then the king kissed Absalom.
